articles.title: SC Stays HC Ruling on GST Portal Upload as Valid Service Under Section 169
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has stayed a High Court judgment that deemed mere uploading of notices and orders on the GST portal as valid communication for appeal purposes. The final legal position on whether portal-based service alone starts the limitation period under Section 107 remains undecided. The case involves the interpretation of service rules under the GST framework.

articles.title: ITAT Quashes Reassessment as Reopening Was Based on Mere Change of Opinion
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed a reassessment order, ruling that reopening a case based solely on a different interpretation of the same evidence amounted to a 'change of opinion.' This decision restricts tax authorities from reopening assessments merely by revisiting prior conclusions. The ruling has implications for tax administration and reassessment procedures in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that reassessment based on a different interpretation of the same material examined during the original assessment amounted to a change of opinion and could not justify reopening. articles.title: Applications to recall ex parte orders filed after an unexplained fourteen-year delay was dismissed
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) dismissed applications to recall an ex parte order dismissed 14 years ago, citing an unexplained delay and lack of sufficient cause for non-appearance. The tribunal ruled that Rule 24 of the ITAT Rules, 1963, does not provide an indefinite timeline for recalling such orders, even with presumed service under the General Clauses Act. The ecosystem facilitates consent-based sharing of financial data, benefiting credit underwriting and customer assessment.

articles.title: No Section 69 Addition as Complete Money Trail From Overseas Remittances Established
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that property investments funded through documented foreign remittances from the assessee’s husband cannot be treated as unexplained income. Bank records and remittance confirmations established the complete source of funds, preventing a Section 69 addition under tax law. This decision sets a precedent for similar cases involving overseas remittances and tax compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that property investment funded through documented foreign remittances from the assessee’s husband could not be treated as unexplained. Bank records and remittance confirmations established the complete source of funds. articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has introduced measures to boost foreign currency inflows, including incentives for external commercial borrowings and bearing hedging costs on FCNR(B) deposits. The central bank also maintained its policy rate at 5.25% and a neutral stance, while revising inflation and growth forecasts.

articles.title: RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, boosts rupee with new measures
articles.summary: The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee unanimously held the repo rate at 5.25% and maintained a neutral stance, citing amplified inflation risks. To bolster the rupee, the central bank introduced multiple measures, including expanding accessible securities for foreign investors and increasing investment limits for NRIs. These actions aim to attract foreign capital and strengthen India's balance of payments.

articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that third-party exports used by EPCG license holders to meet export obligations were valid under the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) framework applicable during the relevant period. The Customs authorities could not disregard the Export Obligation Discharge Certificates (EODCs) restored by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) unless independently proven fraud was established.
articles.raw_summary: Tribunal held that third-party exports relied upon by EPCG licence holders for discharge of export obligation were valid under the FTP framework prevailing during the relevant period and that Customs authorities could not disregard subsisting EODCs restored by DGFT in the absence of independently established fraud. articles.title: APMC Cess Does Not Prove Unaccounted Turnover: ITAT Accepts Commission Agent’s Explanation, But Revives Debtors Verification
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the Assessing Officer incorrectly treated consignment transactions as the assessee's turnover based solely on APMC cess payments. The ITAT accepted the commission agent's explanation that they should be taxed only on commission income, not the consignor's turnover. However, the tribunal revived the verification of debtors for further scrutiny. The ruling clarifies tax obligations for commission agents under India's income tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT found that the Assessing Officer incorrectly treated consignment transactions as the assessees turnover based solely on cess payments. The ruling emphasizes that commission agents should be taxed on commission income and not on consignors turnover. The post APMC Cess Does Not Prove Unaccounted Turnover: ITAT Accepts Commission Agent’s Explanation, But Revives Debtors Verification appeared first on TaxGuru .

He emphasized that any private involvement must not increase healthcare expenses for the public. articles.title: ITAT Denies Section 10(23C)(iiiab) Exemption as Government Grants Were Below 50% Threshold
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a university did not qualify for tax exemption under Section 10(23C)(iiiab) because government grants did not exceed 50% of its total receipts in the relevant year. The tribunal clarified that the 50% threshold must be met for the exemption to apply. This decision impacts educational institutions relying on government funding for tax benefits.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that a university is substantially financed by the Government only when Government grants exceed 50% of total receipts during the relevant previous year. Since the prescribed threshold was not met, the exemption was denied. The post ITAT Denies Section 10(23C)(iiiab) Exemption as Government Grants Were Below 50% Threshold appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Rajasthan HC Quashes Penalty against ITO As Error of Judgment Is Not Misconduct
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court quashed a penalty imposed on an Income Tax Officer (ITO), ruling that an error in judgment does not constitute misconduct unless there is evidence of bad faith or lack of integrity. The court held that a quasi-judicial decision cannot attract disciplinary action simply because another interpretation was possible. The ruling clarifies the threshold for misconduct in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that an Assessing Officer’s quasi-judicial decision cannot attract disciplinary action merely because another view was possible. Absence of mala fides or lack of integrity defeated the misconduct charge. The post Rajasthan HC Quashes Penalty against ITO As Error of Judgment Is Not Misconduct appeared first on TaxGuru .

This ruling impacts how tax authorities conduct reassessments and may influence future policy or administrative practices in India's tax regime.

The case involves ITC and supplier compliance, with broader implications for tax policy and constitutional validity. The ruling keeps the debate alive, leaving unresolved questions about the legality of denying ITC based on supplier actions.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court declined immediate intervention but expressly left open the challenge to the validity of Section 16(2) of the CGST Act. The key takeaway is that constitutional questions relating to denial of ITC due to supplier default remain unresolved. articles.title: Section 43B Addition Quashed as GST Liability Was Shown Only as Liability & not claimed as expense
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that Section 43B of the Income Tax Act cannot be applied if a taxpayer does not claim GST liability as a deductible expense in their profit and loss account. The tribunal deleted the disallowance of such GST liability, as it was only shown as a liability and not claimed as an expense. This decision impacts how businesses account for GST liabilities in their tax filings.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that Section 43B cannot be invoked where the assessee has not claimed the GST liability as a deduction. Since the amount was not debited to the profit and loss account, the disallowance was deleted. The post Section 43B Addition Quashed as GST Liability Was Shown Only as Liability   not claimed as expense appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: LIDW 2026 | ‘A Sanctions Designation in Washington Can Trigger a Treaty Claim in Singapore’: Commercial Disputes in an Age of Geopolitical Fracture
articles.summary: At the London International Disputes Week (LIDW) 2026, legal practitioners examined how geopolitical events like sanctions can disrupt contracts and trigger investment treaty claims. They noted that existing legal tools such as force majeure and MAC clauses have rarely succeeded in pure geopolitical disputes. The discussion highlighted the potential for sanctions imposed by one country to lead to treaty claims in another, underscoring the growing intersection of geopolitics and commercial law.
articles.raw_summary: Force majeure. MAC clauses. Investment treaty claims. Quantum valuations. Practitioners at LIDW 2026 went through the legal toolkit available when geopolitics fractures a contract and found that almost none of it has yet succeeded in a decided case for a pure geopolitical event. The post LIDW 2026 | ‘A Sanctions Designation in Washington Can Trigger a Treaty Claim in Singapore : Commercial Disputes in an Age of Geopolitical Fracture appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: Courts May Use AI, Judges Retain Control: Inside Supreme Court’s Draft AI Regulations
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India has released draft regulations for the use of Artificial Intelligence in courts by 2026. The rules aim to enhance efficiency while ensuring that core judicial functions like adjudication and sentencing remain under human control. The regulations emphasize trust, transparency, and accountability in the integration of AI tools within the judicial process.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has released the draft Regulations for Use of Artificial Intelligence in Courts, 2026, allowing AI to improve efficiency while firmly reserving adjudication, sentencing, and credibility assessment as core judicial functions, grounded in trust, transparency, and institutional accountability. articles.title: Tamil Nadu to end highway contract commission culture, says Minister Aadhav Arjuna
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's government, led by Minister Aadhav Arjuna, has announced major reforms to its highway contracting system. The changes include ending commission-based practices, implementing automated billing and faster payments, and barring benami companies from road projects. These measures aim to improve transparency, attract more infrastructure investment, and speed up the execution of road projects across the state.

The reforms are aimed at improving transparency, attracting infrastructure investment, and ensuring timely execution of road projects across the state.

articles.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has detected methane on the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS for the first time. The comet was first identified in 2023 and is only the third known interstellar object to pass through our solar system.

articles.title: CESTAT Allows Cenvat Credit on Housekeeping Services as They Are Essential for Telecom Operations
articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that housekeeping services used for maintaining telecom equipment and business premises qualify as input services for telecom operators. The tribunal allowed Cenvat credit on these services, stating they are directly connected to the provision of telecommunication output services. The decision impacts tax credit claims for telecom companies under India's service tax regime.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that housekeeping services used for maintaining telecom equipment and business premises qualify as input services. Credit was allowed as the services were directly connected with the provision of telecommunication output services. The post CESTAT Allows Cenvat Credit on Housekeeping Services as They Are Essential for Telecom Operations appeared first on TaxGuru .

As a result, the long-term capital gains addition was deleted. The ruling hinges on the prospective nature of the Section 55A amendment, granting relief to the taxpayer involved.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that the Assessing Officer could not validly refer the valuation issue to the DVO for Assessment Year 2012-13. articles.title: ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Not Valid
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Surat ruled that the Assessing Officer could not refer a property valuation to the Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) when the taxpayer had already provided a higher valuation based on a registered valuer’s report. As a result, the addition made to the taxpayer’s long-term capital gains (LTCG) was deleted. The decision clarifies procedural rules under Section 55A of the Income Tax Act, impacting how tax authorities handle valuation disputes.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Surat held that the Assessing Officer could not refer the property valuation to the DVO where the assessee had adopted a higher value based on a registered valuer’s report. The LTCG addition was deleted. The post ITAT Deletes LTCG Addition as DVO Reference Under Section 55A Was Not Valid appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Foreign Tax Credit Cannot Be Rejected as Late Form 67 Filing Does Not Bar Relief: ITAT Surat
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Surat ruled that foreign tax credit (FTC) cannot be denied to taxpayers solely because they filed Form 67 after the deadline. The tribunal clarified that the filing requirement is procedural and does not affect the taxpayer's substantive right to claim FTC. This decision impacts how tax relief for foreign taxes paid is administered in India.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT held that FTC cannot be denied solely because Form No. 67 was filed after the due date. It ruled that the filing requirement is procedural and does not extinguish the taxpayer’s substantive right to foreign tax credit. This decision impacts tax administration and reassessment procedures in India.

This decision impacts tax administration and reassessment procedures in India, emphasizing the need for accurate and reasoned justification in tax proceedings.

articles.title: Supreme Court releases draft regulations on AI use in courts, invites public feedback
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India has released draft regulations governing the use of artificial intelligence in courts. The draft invites public feedback before finalization. This marks a proactive step by the judiciary to integrate AI while ensuring accountability in legal processes.

articles.summary: The Customs, Excise and Service Tax Appellate Tribunal (CESTAT) ruled that export turnover cannot be added to the value of goods sent for job work when calculating credit reversal under Rule 6. This decision affects tax credit mechanisms for businesses engaged in job work under India's indirect tax framework.

The change aims to streamline compliance for directors while maintaining regulatory oversight. Failure to comply may result in penalties or legal consequences.

articles.title: Director KYC Reset: What Changed, When You File Next and Traps to Avoid
articles.summary: The 2025 amendment to India's corporate governance rules changes how directors file their KYC details. Previously, directors had to file annually under DIR-3 KYC, but now they only need to do so once every three financial years. However, they must still report any changes in their details within prescribed timelines. The change aims to reduce compliance burden while maintaining oversight. Directors should be aware of the new triennial filing cycle and avoid common mistakes in the process.
articles.raw_summary: The 2025 amendment replaces annual DIR-3 KYC filings with a triennial compliance framework. Directors now need to file KYC once every three financial years while continuing to report changes in particulars within prescribed timelines. The post Director KYC Reset: What Changed, When You File Next and Traps to Avoid appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Uttar Pradesh electricity bill surcharge put on hold as regulator steps in
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh Electricity Regulatory Commission has put on hold a proposed surcharge that would have increased electricity bills by nearly 10% for consumers in June. The decision follows intervention by the regulator, and the matter will be revisited after the Uttar Pradesh Power Corporation Limited (UPPCL) submits its response. The proposed surcharge would have directly impacted household electricity costs in the state.
articles.raw_summary: If the proposed surcharge was implemented there would have been an almost 10% hike in June bills for Uttar Pradesh consumers. The matter will be taken up again after UPPCL submits its response before the regulatory body.

articles.title: TMC vs TMC: 59 rebel MLAs back suspended Ritabrata Banerjee as legislature party leader, party dissolves all panels
articles.summary: A faction of 59 MLAs from the Trinamool Congress (TMC) in West Bengal has backed suspended leader Ritabrata Banerjee as the legislature party leader, defying the party's decision. The TMC has dissolved all legislative panels in response. The dispute involves the anti-defection law, which requires two-thirds support (54 MLAs) to avoid disqualification for a breakaway faction.
articles.raw_summary: Under the anti-defection law, a breakaway faction requires the support of at least two-thirds of a legislature party to avoid disqualification. With the TMC having 80 MLAs in the Assembly, the threshold stands at 54.

articles.title: Solar power curtailment raises red flags for renewable investors
articles.summary: On 1 May, nearly one-sixth of India's 150GW solar capacity was curtailed, likely due to low industrial demand during Labour Day. Experts suggest the shutdown of industries for the holiday contributed to the excess power supply. The incident raises concerns for renewable energy investors about grid management and policy support for solar power. The government or regulators may need to address these challenges to ensure stable renewable energy growth.
articles.raw_summary: The solar capacity that was curtailed on 1 May equals almost one-sixth of the country’s installed capacity of 150GW. Experts said that low demand due to industries closing for Labour Day on 1 May may have contributed to the curtailment.

articles.title: Crude oil prices above $100 a barrel: How can it impact your monthly budget?
articles.summary: Crude oil prices have risen above $100 per barrel, affecting India's economy. Higher fuel costs could increase daily commuting expenses, airfare, grocery bills, and dining out. Indirectly, prices of goods made from crude oil derivatives may also rise. The article highlights the broader economic implications for households and businesses.
articles.raw_summary: Higher crude oil prices can impact the monthly budget directly with higher daily commuting costs, costlier vacation flight tickets, higher bills for grocery purchases, and expensive dining out. The indirect impact can come from higher prices of finished goods made using crude oil derivatives.

articles.title: Bulk deposit pricing to be more transparent, flexible
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is introducing new rules for bank deposit interest rates. Banks must now publish their interest rate schedules online each morning and stick to them throughout the day. This ensures customers know the exact rates available. Additionally, banks can now consider withdrawal risk when setting rates for large deposits, adding a fourth factor to pricing.

Judges will retain sole authority over rulings, and lawyers must disclose any AI use in their filings. The framework aims to improve efficiency while preserving judicial independence and due process.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has proposed new rules for artificial intelligence in courts. These rules allow AI for tasks like legal research and drafting. However, AI will not be used to decide court cases. Judges will remain the sole decision-makers. Lawyers must disclose AI use in their filings. This framework aims to enhance efficiency while upholding judicial authority and due process.

This initiative is expected to benefit farmers and save foreign exchange.

articles.title: India to surpass China in share of global GDP by 2060: Report
articles.summary: India is set to overtake China in global economic power by 2060. A new report indicates China's GDP share will decline in the latter half of this century. The global economic landscape is undergoing significant transformation.

articles.title: Service Tax payable on Wall Rent as It Is Integral Part of Advertising Service: CESTAT Chandigarh
articles.summary: The CESTAT Chandigarh ruled that rent paid for walls used to display advertisements is part of the taxable value for advertising services under service tax. The tribunal rejected the advertiser's claim of acting as a pure agent. However, the value of separately sold printed flexes was excluded from service tax valuation. The ruling clarifies the tax treatment of wall rentals in advertising contracts.
articles.raw_summary: CESTAT held that rent paid for walls used to display advertisements forms an intrinsic component of advertising services and must be included in the taxable value. The Tribunal rejected the claim that the advertiser acted as a pure agent. However, the value of printed flexes sold separately was excluded from service tax valuation. articles.title: Widow Cannot Sell Entire Inherited Property as Karta: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court held that heirs inheriting property under Section 8 of the Hindu Succession Act take definite shares as tenants-in-common and not as joint tenants. articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that fantasy sports and online gaming involving monetary stakes on uncertain outcomes are subject to Goods and Services Tax (GST) as they fall under betting and gambling. articles.title: What is El Niño and how could it affect weather this year?
articles.summary: A strong El Niño event, termed a 'super El Niño,' is expected this year, which could cause extreme weather patterns like heavy rainfall and worsening droughts. The phenomenon is a natural climate cycle that affects global weather systems. Such events often prompt governments to prepare for climate-related disasters and adjust policies for agriculture and water management.
articles.raw_summary: A strong El Niño this year, colloquially dubbed a super El Niño, could lead to extreme weather events — from heavy rainfall to exacerbated droughts.

articles.title: SSRI antidepressants are often 'carelessly prescribed'
articles.summary: SSRIs like Prozac and Zoloft are widely prescribed antidepressants, but some experts argue they are overused. The article highlights concerns about careless prescribing practices and the need for better regulation. While not specific to India, the discussion on drug overprescription could influence global or domestic healthcare policies.
articles.raw_summary: SSRIs, like Prozac and Zoloft, are among the world's most-prescribed psychiatric drugs. These antidepressants help a lot of people. But they don't always work. In fact, some experts suggest they are overprescribed.

articles.title: Moderate quake hits Himachal districts; tremors felt in neighbouring States
articles.summary: A moderate earthquake struck near Dharamsala in Himachal Pradesh, about 40 km from the Kangra-Chamba border, at 10:04 pm. The tremors were felt in neighboring states. The earthquake had a depth of 5 km, with the epicenter located near Dhar Godoi in Kangra district. No immediate damage or casualties were reported.

articles.title: SC’s expanding definition of ‘family’—how married daughters won, one judgement at a time
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that marital status should not determine dependency or financial need in legal definitions of 'family'. This decision strengthens the rights of married daughters by removing discriminatory barriers in inheritance and welfare considerations. The ruling builds on previous judgments, progressively expanding family definitions to ensure gender justice. The Court emphasized that such distinctions lack rational basis, reinforcing constitutional principles of equality.
articles.raw_summary: In latest ruling Tuesday, Supreme Court noted that ‘marital status bears no rational nexus’ to considerations like dependency or financial need.

The government's adjustment reflects concerns over potential agricultural impacts due to weaker monsoon rains.

articles.title: Parimatch probe takes ED to Zepto's doorstep
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) is probing offshore betting platform Parimatch for alleged money laundering. As part of the investigation, the agency questioned Zepto, a quick commerce app, about its promotional campaigns for the banned betting app. Investigators found Parimatch used Zepto's delivery flyers to reach households. This probe could lead to stricter regulations or enforcement against illegal betting operations in India.

articles.title: NTPC seeks technology solutions partners for its sub-critical thermal units to operate at lower load
articles.summary: NTPC, India's largest power utility, is inviting technology partners to help its sub-critical thermal power units (150-250 MW) operate at lower loads. The goal is to enable these units to function in two shifts with a minimum technical load of 25%. This move aims to improve efficiency and flexibility in India's thermal power sector, which is crucial for energy security.

The situation could impact India's economic outlook, influencing policy decisions on inflation and energy security.

The economists highlighted these factors as buffers against external shocks.
articles.raw_summary: A panel of economists comprising Aditi Nayar, Chief Economist at ICRA, Sakshi Gupta, Vice President and Senior Economist at HDFC Bank, and Rajani Sinha, Chief Economist at CareEdge Ratings, said the robust performance of the services sector, healthy consumption and stronger investment activity have provided a cushion against external shocks and could help limit the downside risks to growth in FY27.

articles.title: HC junks contempt plea, upholds right of press to report on court order before it’s signed by judge
articles.summary: A division bench of the High Court dismissed a contempt plea against three newspapers that reported on an oral court order before it was formally signed by the judge. The single-judge bench had previously initiated contempt proceedings against the newspapers for reporting the oral order. The division bench upheld the press's right to report on court proceedings, reinforcing media freedom and the public's right to information.
articles.raw_summary: Three newspapers got clean chit from division bench after single-judge bench took exception to reporting of oral court order   initiated contempt proceedings.

articles.title: Vedanta says FEMA-related ED search concludes with no adverse impact

The resignation may lead to discussions or changes in how the state manages its urban development policies.

articles.summary: OPEC's crude oil production fell by 1.22 million barrels per day, the lowest in 37 years, primarily due to a significant drop in Iran's output. articles.title: Airbus delays XLR deliveries to IndiGo as war hits suppliers
articles.summary: Airbus has delayed the delivery of A321XLR aircraft to IndiGo, with only two of the nine ordered jets expected to arrive this year. The delay, caused by supply chain disruptions from the war, threatens IndiGo's plans to expand its long-haul operations amid rising fuel costs and route suspensions. The situation may prompt discussions on aviation sector support or trade-related measures to mitigate such disruptions in the future.

articles.title: Anthropic says AI labs need coordinated plan to halt development if risks rise

This raises concerns about the governance and regulation of advanced AI systems, which could have implications for global and domestic policy frameworks.

articles.title: Anthropic wants AI development to slow down globally, warns humans could lose control otherwise

This raises questions about the need for regulatory oversight and policy measures in AI development.

He advocates for a shift towards equities, citing their better risk-reward profile despite global uncertainty.

articles.title: UN food agency says millions are being pushed into hunger by Iran war
articles.summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) has warned that the ongoing war in Iran and rising global oil prices are worsening food insecurity in countries like Somalia, Afghanistan, and Sri Lanka. High oil prices are also contributing to the crisis by increasing food production and transportation costs.

articles.summary: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a massive fire engulfed a B B hotel in Delhi's Malviya Nagar on Wednesday. Firefighters rescued 37 occupants, while 39 victims were taken to Max Hospital, where 18 were declared dead and 15 remain in intensive care. Authorities said the hotel lacked a mandatory fire NOC, and police have launched a manhunt for the owner while investigating possible safety violations. Foreign nationals accounted for 17 of the 21 fatalities. Prime Mini

This event is seen as a boost to India's tiger conservation efforts under existing wildlife protection policies.

OIL is now undertaking further studies and plans appraisal wells to evaluate the block's potential.

articles.title: The climate crisis looks different for vulnerable children
articles.summary: The article by Sumanta Kar, CEO of SOS Children’s Villages, highlights how the climate crisis disproportionately affects vulnerable children in India. It emphasizes the need for policy measures to address their unique challenges. The piece underscores the intersection of environmental degradation and child rights, calling for systemic solutions.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Sumanta Kar, CEO, SOS Children’s Villages.

articles.title: Navigating last mile of the HPV vaccination programme
articles.summary: This article by Dr Ravi Mehrotra and Abantika Ghosh examines the challenges in the last mile of India's HPV vaccination programme. It discusses the importance of the programme for public health and the obstacles in ensuring widespread coverage. The authors highlight the need for better strategies to improve vaccination rates across the country.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Dr Ravi Mehrotra and Abantika Ghosh.

articles.title: Social-ecological systems perspective needed for dealing with transboundary water conflicts
articles.summary: The article, authored by Md Sarwar Hossain, associate professor of Environmental Science & Sustainability at the University of Glasgow, argues that transboundary water conflicts need to be addressed through a social-ecological systems perspective. It emphasizes the interconnectedness of human and environmental factors in managing shared water resources, which are critical for regions like South Asia where multiple countries depend on the same rivers.
